title: Primitive Taxonomy Entry v1.2
description: Schema for a single leaf-function primitive in the systems engineering taxonomy. Each primitive is an atomic, independently implementable unit of software capability.

primitive_name.description: Unique kebab-case identifier for the primitive within its category

category.description: Taxonomic category the primitive belongs to (e.g., CATEGORY_03_TRANSFORMS)

operation.description: One-sentence description of what the primitive does. Self-contained; no references to other primitives or categories.

signature_shape.description: Parameterized type signature using generic type placeholders: ReturnType function_name(ParamType1, ParamType2). No concrete types. Legend: B (byte-sequence), N (number), S (scalar), C (collection), P (predicate), H (handle), A (address/region), K (key), V (value), T (timestamp), R (result/status), L (path/locator), M (mode/flag-set), D (format/grammar-descriptor), F (callable), E (encoding), void.

state_required.description: Whether the primitive requires state to function: no (stateless, pure function), per-call (needs input/context per invocation), session (needs state maintained across calls within a session), persistent (needs durable storage across sessions)

determinism.description: Determinism of output given identical inputs: deterministic (same input always yields same output), probabilistic (output varies by chance), entropy-dependent (output varies based on external entropy source), external-state-dependent (output depends on OS/runtime state — clocks, process lifecycle, scheduler, environment)

platform.description: Minimum platform requirement: stdlib (language standard library, cross-platform), posix (POSIX-compliant systems), win32 (Windows API), darwin (macOS/iOS specific), lib-ext (requires third-party library)

decomposable.description: Whether this primitive can be built from simpler primitives in the taxonomy

decomposes_to.description: List of simpler primitive names this can be decomposed into. Omit when decomposable is false.

depends_on.description: List of primitives this calls internally at runtime. Empty array means no internal dependencies.

isolation.description: Recommended execution isolation boundary: none (runs in caller context), process (should run in separate process), thread (separate thread sufficient), sandbox (requires restricted execution environment)

ownership.description: Resource ownership model: caller (caller owns all side effects), callee (primitive manages its own resources), shared (both caller and callee share responsibility)
